note: Mathlib/Analysis/Complex/Harmonic/Poisson.lean:102 -- Poisson-kernel reproducing formula: for f already HarmonicContOnCl on a ball, f(w) = circleAverage(poissonKernel c w * f) c R. This is the representation-formula machinery adjacent to the classical Dirichlet problem (existence of a harmonic extension of arbitrary continuous boundary data on a disk), but it assumes f is already harmonic on the whole closed disk -- Mathlib has no construction of the Poisson integral of a boundary function nor an existence/uniqueness theorem for the boundary-value problem itself (grepped for poissonIntegral and dirichlet near this code: no hits).
verify_note: Decl InnerProductSpace.HarmonicContOnCl.circleAverage_poissonKernel_smul confirmed at Poisson.lean:102 (theorem HarmonicContOnCl.circleAverage_poissonKernel_smul under namespace InnerProductSpace), in declaration-data.json cache and mathlib4 checkout; QID Q1192869 confirmed as Dirichlet problem via Wikidata; the decl proves f(w) = circleAverage(poissonKernel c w smul f) c R for f already HarmonicContOnCl on the ball, i.e. the reproducing/mean-value half of the classical Poisson solution, NOT the existence/uniqueness construction from arbitrary continuous boundary data -- match_kind related (not exact) is honest, and grep confirms no poissonIntegral/dirichlet existence theorem exists elsewhere in Mathlib to compete for the exact claim.
